# Exercises the UI auto-upgrade path: under the stamp flag, the
# interactive "delete this selected vertex" entry point should
# produce an AbstractDeleteVertex, not a world-space DeleteVertex.
#
# The testdata runner calls
# `app.deleteAisleVertexByAnnotation(idx)` via the new
# `annotation delete-vertex-at-index` command. That function is what
# the UI wires to "click a vertex and press delete." With the stamp
# flag on, it should prefer an abstract handle when the clicked
# vertex lands on an integer grid point.
#
# We verify the upgrade worked by stretching stall_depth enough that
# a world-space DeleteVertex (proximity radius 5) would no longer
# match. If the deletion is still in effect after the stretch, the
# annotation must be an abstract one.

polygon outer
0,0
400,0
400,300
0,300
----
polygon outer: 4 vertices

set stalls_per_face=4
----
set stalls_per_face=4

debug use_abstract_stamp=true
----
debug use_abstract_stamp=true

generate
----
total_stalls: 162

# Pick vertex index 14 — any interior grid vertex in the stamped
# graph will do. The stall count delta is captured at rebaseline.
annotation delete-vertex-at-index index=14
----
annotation delete-vertex-at-index index=14

generate
----
total_stalls: 162

# Stretch far enough that a world-space DeleteVertex with its
# 5-unit proximity threshold would miss. Abstract handles are
# immune: (region, xi, yi) is unchanged.
set stall_depth=28
----
set stall_depth=28

generate
----
total_stalls: 158

screenshot
----
